name: walk_next_external_body_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_1_walk_next_external_body_unconstrained(mem: PTMem, walk1: Walk, walk2: Walk)
    requires
        walk1.vaddr == walk2.vaddr,
        walk1.path == walk2.path,
        walk1.complete == walk2.complete,
    ensures
        walk_next(mem, walk1) == walk_next(mem, walk2),
{
}
reason: `walk_next` is `external_body` with `unimplemented!()` — completely opaque, so identical inputs could produce different outputs

name: lemma_aux1_external_body_path_equality
reason: `lemma_iter_walk_result_vbase_equal_aux1` is `external_body` — the claimed path equality between walking from the result vaddr and the original walk is trusted without proof

name: lemma_aux2_external_body_vaddr_idempotent
reason: `lemma_iter_walk_result_vbase_equal_aux2` is `external_body` — the vaddr idempotency property is trusted without proof

name: flags_eagerly_computed_beyond_path
code:
proof fn phi_4_flags_eagerly_computed_beyond_path(walk: Walk)
    requires
        walk.path.len() == 2,
        walk.path[0].1 is Directory,
        walk.path[1].1 is Page,
    ensures
        walk.flags() == Flags::from_GPDE(walk.path[0].1).combine(Flags::from_GPDE(walk.path[1].1)),
{
}
reason: `Walk::flags` eagerly indexes `path[2]` and `path[3]` even when path.len() == 2 — the computation of `flags2` and `flags3` reads beyond the path but the final result selects `flags1`, relying on Verus not triggering the out-of-bounds access in spec mode

name: walk_result_invalid_aligns_to_page
code:
proof fn phi_5_walk_result_invalid_aligns_to_page(walk: Walk)
    requires
        walk.path.len() > 0,
        walk.path.last().1 is Invalid,
        walk.complete,
        walk.vaddr % PAGE_SIZE as usize == 0,
    ensures
        walk.result() is Invalid,
        walk.result()->Invalid_vaddr == walk.vaddr,
{
}
reason: An invalid walk result aligns the vaddr to PAGE_SIZE — for already page-aligned vaddrs this is identity, but for non-aligned vaddrs the reported Invalid address differs from the actual queried address
